goal: Implement Aether Skills Layer — smart-matched colony and domain skills for workers
decisions: Skills inject outside colony-prime as separate skill_section with 12K budget
OpenCode command parity required for lint:sync (hard gate)
New bash tests need explicit npm script (test:skills) — not auto-discovered
Watcher prompt in build-verify.md also needs skill injection
Autopilot uses same playbooks — no separate code path to maintain

learnings.claim: Process substitution (< <(cmd)) avoids subshell variable loss in while loops piped from jq — critical for accumulating results in bash
learnings.evidence: 19 bash tests pass including scoring loops that modify variables inside while loops

learnings.claim: TDD with background agents works well — write tests first in one agent, implementation in parallel agent, then merge results
learnings.evidence: Forge-1 wrote 9 failing tests while Anvil-2 built skills.sh; all tests passed on first wire-up

learnings.claim: Parallel agents for content authoring (colony vs domain) is highly efficient — independent file sets with no conflicts
learnings.evidence: 28 SKILL.md files created by 2 parallel builders with zero file conflicts

learnings.claim: Playbook modifications (markdown instruction files) can safely be parallelized when each agent edits a different file — no merge conflicts
learnings.evidence: 3 agents edited build-context.md, build-wave.md, and build-verify.md simultaneously with zero conflicts
